title: Subject-To Purchase Agreement
description: A contract where the buyer purchases the property subject to the existing mortgage remaining in place.
whatsCovered: Definition of professional subject-to transactions, mortgage assumptions, and financing risks
Security deposits, escrow rules, and payment milestones
Property access, landlord entry, and keys protocols
Disclosures, lead paint compliance, and state law notices
Standard governing law and contract enforcement guidelines
whenToUse: Use this contract when organizing transactions or agreements for subject-to transactions, mortgage assumptions, and financing risks to maintain complete legal compliance with state property regulations.

clauses.heading: SUBJECT-TO PURCHASE AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This Real Estate Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {provider_name}, located at {provider_address} ("First Party"), and {client_name}, located at {client_address} ("Second Party").

clauses.heading: 1. Property / Covenants Scope
clauses.body: Buyer purchases the property subject to the existing loan serviced by: {loan_servicer_name}.

clauses.heading: 2. Financial Obligations & Fees
clauses.body: Buyer agrees to make monthly payments directly on the mortgage balance of {existing_mortgage_balance} on behalf of Seller.

clauses.heading: 3. Disclosures and Environmental Covenants
clauses.body: Seller understands that the loan remains in their name and late payments will impact their credit.

clauses.heading: 4. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Is this agreement binding in all US states?
faq.answer: Yes, but real estate laws vary significantly. Ensure you specify the correct governing state in the Legal Settings field before closing.

faq.question: How are security or escrow funds handled?
faq.answer: All security and earnest deposits must be held in dedicated escrow trust accounts in accordance with state license commission laws.
